metadata.title: Cycle Thinking in Investing — Lessons from Losing Money

conclusion: Yongqing joins Wu Xiaobo for 120 minutes on cycle thinking — enterprise lifecycle, industry lifecycle, capital cycle, and sentiment cycle interlock. He maps four loss traps, separates intrinsic value from price, and notes mutual funds peak-weight sectors at tops. Rational valuation must pair with expected return, not narrative heat. BRK.B Long as cycle-discipline compounder; TSM Watch as capital-cycle bellwether through semiconductor capex swings.
background: In a 120-minute cross-show episode, Practical Investments co-founder Yongqing speaks with Wu Xiaobo on cycles. Four lenses: enterprise lifecycle (founder to bureaucracy), industry lifecycle (growth to consolidation), capital cycle (funding gluts and droughts), sentiment cycle (greed/fear overshoot). Four loss traps — buying high on sentiment, holding through fundamental decay, over-levering cyclicals, confusing price with value — recur across eras. Mutual funds historically peak-weight fashionable sectors at tops. Yongqing insists rational valuation must translate to expected return math; BRK.B embodies cycle patience, TSM flags global capital-cycle swings in fabs.
important_facts: Four cycles: enterprise, industry, capital, sentiment — Yongqing and Wu map how all four stack; mistiming one cycle layer multiplies drawdowns. Enterprise lifecycle decay (bureaucracy) often coincides with industry maturity and sentiment peaks at year 10–15 of industry S-curves.

Four loss traps: (1) sentiment-top buying, (2) holding through fundamental decay, (3) cyclical leverage, (4) price-value confusion. Mutual fund peak sector weights at tops are a measurable sentiment indicator — avoid mirroring crowd max allocation.

Valuation discipline: intrinsic value is dynamic, not a static PE label — must connect to expected return under cycle-aware scenarios (12–15% hurdle common in Yongqing's framework). BRK.B Long as patient compounder; TSM Watch on semiconductor capex and capital-cycle turns.

mental_model.name: Four Cycles × Four Traps × Expected Return
mental_model.components: Cycles stack — enterprise, industry, capital, sentiment — mistiming one amplifies losses. Four traps recur: heat-chasing, decay denial, cyclical leverage, price-value confusion. Mutual fund max weights signal tops. Rational valuation equals expected return under cycle scenarios, not slogan cheapness.
mental_model.application: Berkshire (BRK.B): Long — cycle patience and cash optionality when sentiment peaks. Taiwan Semi (TSM): Watch — capital-cycle bellwether; fab capex swings drive earnings variance. Trim when mutual funds peak-weight your sector; add when expected return clears hurdle with intact enterprise lifecycle.

key_insights.view: Cycles stack; missing one layer is expensive.
key_insights.question: What is Yongqing's cycle framework?
key_insights.answer: Enterprise, industry, capital, and sentiment cycles interlock — buying at sentiment peak in a mature industry with tightening capital is the worst stack. Wu Xiaobo collaboration stresses teaching traps, not predicting dates. Map where you are on each cycle before sizing.

key_insights.view: Mutual fund peak weights are contrarian signals.
key_insights.question: How to use fund positioning data?
key_insights.answer: Historic pattern: funds max-weight hot sectors at tops — measurable sentiment extremity. Pair with four loss traps checklist before adding exposure. Expected return must improve as price falls, not just narratives flip bearish.

key_insights.question: How differs from low-PE value labels?
key_insights.answer: Dynamic intrinsic value under cycle scenarios — cash flows, reinvestment, decay risk — must yield hurdle-beating expected return. Price cheap on PE can be value trap if enterprise lifecycle is decaying. BRK.B wins by surviving all four cycles with discipline.

top_investment_implications.thesis: Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B): cycle-discipline compounder — Yongqing Long through enterprise/industry/capital/sentiment stacks; cash optionality at sentiment peaks
Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM): capital-cycle bellwether — fab capex and funding swings drive earnings; Watch for cycle turn alignment with rational expected-return entry

golden_quotes: Enterprise, industry, capital, sentiment — four cycles stack; mistime one and the loss compounds.
Mutual funds peak-weight the hot sector at the top — that is a measurable sentiment signal.
Rational valuation is not a low PE sticker — it is expected return after you price the cycles.

events.event: Zero-rate capital cycle; long enterprise growth phases
Consumer herding peak; mutual funds max weight
Sentiment reset; rate-driven capital cycle tightens
AI sentiment cycle peaks; fund weights concentrate
